failure_surface: Code crashed with AttributeError during verification step: 'Color' object has no attribute 'index_rgb' when attempting to read cell fill color properties from openpyxl Cell object
root_cause: Agent incorrectly assumed the existence of 'index_rgb' attribute on openpyxl Color/fill objects. The verification code attempted to access .fill.start_color.index_rgb which does not exist in openpyxl's API, causing the entire script to fail before the file could be saved.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Remove or simplify verification code that reads back fill colors using non-standard openpyxl attributes. Use only documented openpyxl properties (e.g., .fill.fgColor.rgb) for any color-related checks, or skip detailed verification of style attributes entirely.
evidence: Line 84: fill_color = ws.cell(row=row, column=BL_COL).fill.fgColor.rgb if ws.cell(row=row, column=BL_COL).fill.start_color.index_rgb else 'None'
Error traceback shows: AttributeError: 'Color' object has no attribute 'index_rgb'
Failure occurred during verification/printing phase after main logic had already run
File save operation never completed due to unhandled exception

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL Fill Color Attribute Names
memory_items.lesson: When reading cell fill color in openpyxl, use documented attributes like .fill.fgColor.rgb instead of assuming internal attributes like 'index_rgb' exist
memory_items.generalization: Verify openpyxl Color and PatternFill object properties against official documentation before using them in read operations; avoid guessing at attribute names

memory_items.title: Defensive Verification Code
memory_items.lesson: Verification/logging code should not crash the main workflow even if it contains errors; wrap attribute access in try-except or use simpler checks
memory_items.generalization: When adding post-processing verification or logging, ensure failures in that code do not prevent the core task (file saving, data processing) from completing

memory_items.title: Library-Specific API Assumptions
memory_items.lesson: Do not assume spreadsheet library APIs (openpyxl, pandas, etc.) expose all internal state through intuitive attribute chains
memory_items.generalization: Test library attribute paths in isolation before embedding them into full workflows; use help() or documentation to confirm attribute existence

edits.content: - **Style Verification**: When verifying styles (colors, fonts), use only documented library properties. Do not attempt to read style attributes using inferred/internal property names (e.g., avoid .index_rgb on Color objects). Wrap verification code in error handling so logging failures don't break file saving.
